reason: Builder produced a clean, reusable script (nutrition_app/scripts/fix_plan_variety.py, 326 lines) that independently re-runs and confirms all 7 newest plans satisfy the <=60% rule (max observed share VEG=0.2857 in 2026-05-26_17-23-19_timing_fix.json). No originals were touched (mtimes pre-date the build) and no *_variety_fix.json siblings were created. Criteria 1, 2, 5 pass with direct evidence; criteria 3, 4, 6 are conditional on a swap occurring -- none did, so they are only verifiable by code inspection (which shows correct structural enforcement: single-item swap, KCAL_TOLERANCE=0.05, sibling write + variety_fix provenance block). Marking UNCERTAIN for the swap-conditional criteria per the hard rule against faking confidence. No revert; the build did the right thing for the observed data and does not damage the project.

criterion_results.criterion: The 7 newest plan_*.json files under storage_agents/plans/ each have no food category (PROTEIN, GRAIN, VEG, FRUIT, DAIRY) exceeding 60% of items per day after the fix
criterion_results.evidence: Independent re-run of fix_plan_variety.py confirms all 7 plans have violation=None; max share across all 7 is VEG=0.2857 in 2026-05-26_17-23-19_timing_fix.json. All other shares <=0.2381. Well under the 0.60 threshold.

criterion_results.criterion: Plans are joined against the food catalog to look up category per food_id (plan items do not carry a category field, only food_id/food_name)
criterion_results.evidence: Script loads nutrition_app/data/foods_extended.json into a {food_id: item} dict (load_catalog, line 41-44) and looks up category for each plan item by food_id (compute_category_shares, line 76). Catalog confirmed to contain protein/grain/vegetable/fruit/dairy categories among others.

criterion_results.criterion: Any plan that required a swap has exactly one item replaced; replacement food_id exists in the existing food catalog (no new foods added)
criterion_results.evidence: No plan required a swap in this run. Code review: pick_replacement returns a single (mi, ii, replacement_fid, new_item) tuple and replacement is drawn from catalog.values() via catalog_candidates_for_rule (line 146-148), structurally enforcing the criterion. No empirical evidence available because the trigger never fired.

criterion_results.criterion: After swap, total daily kcal of the plan stays within +/-5% of the pre-swap total
criterion_results.evidence: No swaps performed. Code review: pick_replacement enforces 'if deviation <= KCAL_TOLERANCE' with KCAL_TOLERANCE=0.05 (line 38, 178). Structurally enforced if ever triggered; no execution evidence available.

criterion_results.criterion: Plans that already satisfy the 60% rule are left unmodified (no spurious edits)
criterion_results.evidence: All 7 plans satisfy the rule. find on plans dir for '*_variety_fix.json' returns empty. Original plan files' mtimes (e.g., 2026-05-27 14:26:54 for iter_19_timing_fix.json) pre-date the build run (15:23). No spurious edits.

criterion_results.criterion: Each modified plan is written back as a sibling file with suffix _variety_fix.json (do not overwrite the original) and includes a 'variety_fix' provenance block listing original_item food_id, replacement food_id, and the category share before/after
criterion_results.evidence: No plan required modification, so no sibling file was produced. Code review (process_plan_file, line 248-279): builds plan['variety_fix'] = {original_item:{food_id,...,category_rule}, replacement_item:{food_id,...,category_rule}, category_shares_before, category_shares_after, daily_kcal_before, daily_kcal_after, kcal_deviation_pct, rule_threshold} and writes to plan_path.with_name(stem + '_variety_fix.json'). Structurally satisfies the criterion when triggered; no execution evidence.
